description_original.content:
# Policy Consultant on Green Transition

Application stage: Saved
Compensation: Maximum estimated value €35,000/year excluding VAT; daily fee requested
Deadline: July 14, 2026 9:00 AM (GMT+2)
Engagement: Consultancy
Last reviewed: July 10, 2026
Level: Consultant
Location: Brussels, Belgium
Organisation: Green European Foundation (GEF)
Source documents: Call-for-policy-consultant-on-green-transition.pdf
Start date: August 1, 2026
Themes: Climate, Energy, Policy
Work arrangement: Hybrid

## Purpose

GEF seeks a research and policy adviser focused on EU energy policy, eco-social policy, rights-based approaches, and wellbeing. The provider will produce research, analysis, recommendations, publications, events, and stakeholder engagement while supporting GEF as a hub for green policy expertise.

## Tasks and deliverables

- Conduct qualitative and quantitative policy research on the green transition.
- Liaise with NGOs, think tanks, policymakers, and institutional actors.
- Coordinate a Knowledge Community on participation in the EU energy transition.
- Moderate, take notes, and report on expert roundtables.
- Coordinate external experts and draft or review political briefs.
- Support additional work on EU social policy, the economy, sustainable welfare, and wellbeing.
- Support events, meetings, conferences, representation, and network building.
- Deliver content and financial reporting.

## Contract

- Service-provider arrangement averaging 1.5 days per week in the Brussels office, with telework possible.
- Initial mission from August 2026 to August 2027.
- Renewable up to five years based on quality and performance.
- Maximum estimated annual value: €35,000 excluding VAT.
- Proposal should state a daily fee, VAT rate, and any minimum billable amount.

## Selection criteria

- High-quality qualitative and quantitative policy research.
- Strong expertise and networks in EU energy policy, eco-social policy, rights-based approaches, and wellbeing.
- Science and technology policy background and experience connecting advocacy, policy, and technical analysis.
- Knowledge of EU institutions, policymaking, political dynamics, and diverse green-transition contexts.
- Research design, coordination, and leadership.
- Excellent English policy writing, presentation, public speaking, and facilitation.
- At least five years of relevant policy research or advisory experience.
- Strong stakeholder engagement, organisation, and time management.
- Availability for in-person work in Brussels.

## Award methodology

Best price-quality ratio:

- EU energy and related policy expertise: 30%.
- Project coordination and research leadership: 20%.
- Communication, writing, and dissemination: 20%.
- Financial offer: 30%.

Formula: [(lowest price / tenderer's price) × 30] + [(quality score / 100) × 70].

## Application package

- Introduction covering background, interest, competence, and fit.
- Competitive daily fee in euros excluding VAT, with VAT rate.
- CV and relevant writing, research, and policy samples.
- Exclusion-criteria and conflict-of-interest declaration, or ESPD.
- Submit through the official application form.

Questions: jamie.kendrick@gef.eu

Contracting party: Green European Foundation, Avenue des Arts 7-8, 1210 Brussels.

## Source

Call-for-policy-consultant-on-green-transition.pdf
